canonical_question: What are Informatica IICS capabilities for SAP and Oracle enterprise integration?
aliases: Informatica Cloud Data Integration SAP Oracle connectors
IICS iPaaS capabilities for enterprise ERP integration
Informatica IDMC Secure Agent architecture and connector depth
Informatica IICS vs MuleSoft vs Boomi for SAP Oracle

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Winter 2025 — CLAIRE AI copilot GA, MCP Server announced for April 2026

constraints: IPU-based pricing — all processing metered via Informatica Processing Units; no per-API-call rate limits but overage charges apply
Secure Agent required for on-premise connectivity — cannot directly connect to behind-firewall SAP/Oracle without agent installed on-site
SAP ODP extractor requires SAP ODP framework activation and appropriate RFC authorizations in SAP
Oracle CDC requires redo log access (SYSASM or SYSDBA authority for ASM); LogMiner alternative has performance overhead
CAI process execution capped by IPU allocation — high-frequency real-time APIs consume IPUs rapidly
Marketplace connectors may have separate licensing — not all 400+ connectors included in base IPU subscription

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need a lightweight point-to-point integration without data transformation
Need API-first architecture with full API lifecycle management as primary use case
Only need SAP-to-SAP replication or S/4HANA migration

often_confused_with.label: Informatica PowerCenter — legacy on-premise ETL, not IICS

inputs_needed.question: What integration pattern do you need?
